category_title: ADHD-diagnosed student who seeks social contact and struggles with exclusion and medication-dependent behavior
student_description: Jacob is an ADHD-diagnosed student who deeply desires social connection but struggles with distractibility, impulsivity, and peer rejection. When overwhelmed or unmedicated, he can become disruptive or physically aggressive, which leads to classmates ostracizing him. He uniquely needs explicit teacher mediation to manage peer reactions, structured cooperative tasks to channel his desire to help others, and the option to withdraw to maintain self-control.

criteria.rationale: Jacob naturally wants to observe and help peers; structuring this into cooperative tasks turns a distraction into a productive social interaction.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan incorporates structured cooperative or collaborative activities rather than relying solely on isolated independent work.

criteria.rationale: Jacob becomes easily overwhelmed when he feels he must comprehend everything his peers are doing, which triggers off-task behavior.
criteria.check: The tutor designs activities with less demanding rules or reduced cognitive load to help Jacob focus on positive participation and peer relations.
